name: AutoGen 0.4
description: Microsoft Research's asynchronous, event-driven multi-agent framework. Supports distributed agent networks with a pluggable model client interface.
curator_note: The async event-driven architecture in 0.4 is a major leap. Best for complex multi-agent systems.

name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
description: Anthropic's open standard for connecting AI models to external data sources and tools. Becoming the universal interface layer for agentic systems in 2026.
curator_note: The USB-C of AI tooling. Framework-agnostic and rapidly becoming an industry standard.

name: LangGraph
description: Graph-based orchestration framework from LangChain. Build stateful, multi-actor agent applications with fine-grained control over cycles, branching, and persistence.
curator_note: Production-grade stateful agent orchestration. The graph model solves the 'agent loop' problem cleanly.

name: PydanticAI
description: Agent framework built on Pydantic for type-safe, structured LLM interactions. Integrates natively with FastAPI for production deployment.
curator_note: If you're already on FastAPI, this is the natural choice. Type safety catches bugs before production.
